topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

I found the login details tucked inside an old charging cable in Leo’s backpack. My stomach tightened as I typed them in, the screen illuminating the cluttered desk. It was a private account, clearly. The feed was a curated highlight reel of aesthetic travel photos and cryptic, moody captions. I scrolled through weeks of it, my brow furrowing with confusion. Who was this person? Leo hadn't mentioned any secret online life, not even vaguely. A strange sense of dislocation washed over me; the teenager I knew seemed to have a whole other dimension operating behind a screen. I leaned back in my chair, the silence in the room suddenly heavy, watching the images flicker.

[story 2]

It happened during a routine check of the shared family tablet. A notification popped up—a profile picture I didn't recognize, followed by a stream of posts about obscure video game lore. I clicked, and suddenly, the reality of what I was seeing hit me. It was a fully developed persona, vibrant and engaging, completely separate from the quiet, bookish boy I usually dealt with. My fingers hovered over the 'log out' button, trembling slightly. I stared at the screen, my breath catching in my throat. Why keep this hidden? The silence in the living room amplified the frantic rhythm of my own heartbeat. It felt like uncovering a hidden room in a house I thought I knew intimately.

[story 3]

The discovery wasn't dramatic; it was almost insidious. While searching for a misplaced textbook, I stumbled upon a forgotten password entry on Leo’s phone. Curiosity, sharp and unwelcome, compelled me to explore. The account was dormant for months, yet the content was rich—snippets of witty commentary and behind-the-scenes glimpses of activities I couldn't possibly know about. I reread a caption, my lips moving soundlessly. A slow, disbelieving smile touched my face before it vanished. I closed the app abruptly, the sudden return to the mundane feeling jarring. I looked at Leo, who was engrossed in his phone, and felt an unfamiliar distance between us, as if I were observing a stranger wearing his skin.
